It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n18\t// status codes, pattern-based range validation, and flexible validation patterns.\n19\t//\n20\t// Functions:\n21\t// - ValidateStatusCodeInt: Basic status code validation with expected/actual ints\n22\t// - ValidateStatusCodePattern: Validate status code matches pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n23\t// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code (test helper)\n24\t// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n25\t// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n26\t// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n27\t// - AssertStatusCode: Flexible validation supporting both assertion and boolean modes\n28\t// - AssertStatusCodeAny: Flexible multi-code validation with assertion mode\n29\t// - StatusCodeMatchResult: Detailed validation result with error context\n30\t// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n31\t// - parseStatusCodePattern: Parse pattern string to extract century digit\n32\t// =============================================================================\n33\t\n34\t// ValidateStatusCodeInt validates expected vs actual HTTP status codes.\n35\t//\n36\t// This is a basic status code validation function that compares two integers\n37\t// and returns an error if they don't match. It's useful for simple validation\n38\t// scenarios where you have direct access to status code integers.\n39\t//\n40\t// Parameters:\n41\t//   - expected: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n42\t//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n43\t//\n44\t// Returns:\n45\t//   - nil if the status codes match\n46\t//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n47\t//\n48\t// Example (matching codes):\n49\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 200)\n50\t//   // err == nil\n51\t//\n52\t// Example (non-matching codes):\n53\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 404)\n54\t//   // err.Error() == \"status code mismatch: expected 200 (OK), got 404 (Not Found)\"\n55\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeInt(expected, actual int) error {\n56\t\tif expected == actual {\n57\t\t\treturn nil\n58\t\t}\n59\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status code mismatch: expected %d (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n60\t\t\texpected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected),\n61\t\t\tactual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual))\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t// StatusCodeMatchResult contains detailed information about a status code validation.\n65\t//\n66\t// This type provides comprehensive error context for debugging validation failures,\n67\t// including the expected and actual status codes with their descriptions, the\n68\t// response object context, and a formatted error message.\n69\ttype StatusCodeMatchResult struct {\n70\t\t// Match indicates whether the status code matched successfully\n71\t\tMatch bool\n72\t\n73\t\t// Expected is the status code that was expected\n74\t\tExpected int\n75\t\n76\t\t// Actual is the status code that was received\n77\t\tActual int\n78\t\n79\t\t// ExpectedDescription is the human-readable description of the expected status code\n80\t\tExpectedDescription string\n81\t\n82\t\t// ActualDescription is the human-readable description of the actual status code\n83\t\tActualDescription string\n84\t\n85\t\t// ResponseContext contains information about the response object\n86\t\tResponseContext string\n87\t\n88\t\t// Error is a formatted error message describing the mismatch\n89\t\tError string\n90\t}\n91\t\n92\t// String returns a human-readable representation of the validation result.\n93\tfunc (r StatusCodeMatchResult) String() string {\n94\t\tif r.Match {\n95\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code match: %d (%s)\", r.Actual, r.ActualDescription)\n96\t\t}\n97\t\treturn r.Error\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\t// AssertStatusCode validates status code with flexible assertion mode.\n101\t//\n102\t// This function supports both boolean-only mode (for conditional logic) and\n103\t// assertion mode (with descriptive error messages). When assertMode is true\n104\t// and validation fails, it returns a detailed error message showing expected\n105\t// vs actual values along with response context.\n106\t//\n107\t// Parameters:\n108\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n109\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n110\t//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure; if false, return bool only\n111\t//\n112\t// Returns:\n113\t//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n114\t//\n115\t// Example (boolean mode):\n116\t//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 200, false)\n117\t//   if !result.Match {\n118\t//       // Handle non-200 case\n119\t//   }\n120\t//\n121\t// Example (assertion mode):\n122\t//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 404, true)\n123\t//   if !result.Match {\n124\t//       t.Error(result.Error)  // Full error with expected vs actual\n125\t//   }\n126\tfunc AssertStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n127\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n128\t\n129\t\t// Build response context for error messages\n130\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n131\t\n132\t\t// Check if status codes match\n133\t\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n134\t\n135\t\tif matches {\n136\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n137\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n138\t\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n139\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n140\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n141\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n142\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n143\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n144\t\t\t}\n145\t\t}\n146\t\n147\t\t// Build detailed error message\n148\t\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchError(\n149\t\t\texpectedCode,\n150\t\t\tactualCode,\n151\t\t\tresponseContext,\n152\t\t)\n153\t\n154\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n155\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n156\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n157\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n158\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n159\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n160\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n161\t\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n162\t\t}\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\t// AssertStatusCodeAny validates status code against multiple allowed codes.\n166\t//\n167\t// This function is similar to AssertStatusCode but accepts multiple allowed\n168\t// status codes. Returns detailed error information when validation fails,\n169\t// including the full list of allowed status codes.\n170\t//\n171\t// Parameters:\n172\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n173\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable status codes\n174\t//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure\n175\t//\n176\t// Returns:\n177\t//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n178\t//\n179\t// Example:\n180\t//   result := AssertStatusCodeAny(w, []int{200, 201, 204}, true)\n181\t//   if !result.Match {\n182\t//       t.Error(result.Error)\n183\t//   }\n184\tfunc AssertStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n185\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n186\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n187\t\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n188\t\t\t\tExpected:           0,\n189\t\t\t\tActual:             0,\n190\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: \"\",\n191\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   \"\",\n192\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     getStatusCodeResponseContext(response),\n193\t\t\t\tError:              \"AssertStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\",\n194\t\t\t}\n195\t\t}\n196\t\n197\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n198\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n199\t\n200\t\t// Check if actual code matches any in the allowed list\n201\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n202\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n203\t\t\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n204\t\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n205\t\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n206\t\t\t\t\tExpected:           actualCode,\n207\t\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n208\t\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n209\t\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n210\t\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n211\t\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n212\t\t\t\t}\n213\t\t\t}\n214\t\t}\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build helpful error message showing what was allowed vs. what was received\n217\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n218\t\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(\n219\t\t\tallowedDesc,\n220\t\t\tactualCode,\n221\t\t\tresponseContext,\n222\t\t)\n223\t\n224\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n225\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n226\t\t\tExpected:           0,\n227\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n228\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n229\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n230\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n231\t\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n232\t\t}\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchError creates a detailed error message for status code mismatches.\n236\t//\n237\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message that includes:\n238\t// - The expected status code with description\n239\t// - The actual status code with description\n240\t// - Response object context (type and status if available)\n241\tfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n242\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n243\t\n244\t\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n245\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", expected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected)))\n246\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n247\t\n248\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n249\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n250\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\treturn b.String()\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n256\t//\n257\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when validating against multiple\n258\t// allowed status codes, showing all allowed codes vs the actual received code.\n259\tfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedList string, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n260\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n261\t\n262\t\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n263\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: one of [%s]\\n\", allowedList))\n264\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n265\t\n266\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n267\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n268\t\t}\n269\t\n270\t\treturn b.String()\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n274\t//\n275\t// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n276\t// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\n277\tfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n278\t\tswitch response.(type) {\n279\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n280\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n281\t\tcase *http.Response:\n282\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n283\t\tdefault:\n284\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n285\t\t}\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n289\t//\n290\t// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n291\t// including their descriptions for better debugging.\n292\tfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n293\t\tvar parts []string\n294\t\tfor _, code := range codes {\n295\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n296\t\t}\n297\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n301\t//\n302\t// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n303\t// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n304\t// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n305\t//\n306\t// Parameters:\n307\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n308\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n309\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n310\t//\n311\t// Example:\n312\t//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n313\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n314\t\tt.Helper()\n315\t\n316\t\tresult := AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, true)\n317\t\n318\t\tif !result.Match {\n319\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: %d (%s)\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n320\t\t\t\tresult.Expected, result.ExpectedDescription,\n321\t\t\t\tresult.Actual, result.ActualDescription,\n322\t\t\t\tresult.ResponseContext)\n323\t\t}\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n327\t//\n328\t// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n329\t// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n330\t// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n331\t//\n332\t// Parameters:\n333\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n334\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n335\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n336\t//\n337\t// Example:\n338\t//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\n339\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n340\t\tt.Helper()\n341\t\n342\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n343\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n344\t\t\treturn\n345\t\t}\n346\t\n347\t\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n348\t\n349\t\tif !result.Match {\n350\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n351\t\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n352\t\t}\n353\t}\n354\t\n355\t// ValidateStatusCodePattern validates that an HTTP status code matches a pattern.\n356\t//\n357\t// This function validates status codes using pattern strings like '4xx', '5xx', etc.\n358\t// It extracts the century digit from the pattern and validates that the actual code\n359\t// falls within the corresponding range (e.g., '4xx' validates codes 400-499).\n360\t//\n361\t// Parameters:\n362\t//   - pattern: The status code pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx')\n363\t//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n364\t//\n365\t// Returns:\n366\t//   - nil if the status code matches the pattern\n367\t//   - error with descriptive message if pattern is invalid or code doesn't match\n368\t//\n369\t// Example (matching pattern):\n370\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 404)\n371\t//   // err == nil\n372\t//\n373\t// Example (non-matching pattern):\n374\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 200)\n375\t//   // err.Error() == \"status code 200 (OK) does not match pattern '4xx' (expected 400-499)\"\n376\t//\n377\t// Example (invalid pattern):\n378\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('invalid', 200)\n379\t//   // err.Error() == \"invalid status code pattern 'invalid': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\"\n380\tfunc ValidateStatusCodePattern(pattern string, actual int) error {\n381\t\t// Parse the pattern to extract the century digit\n382\t\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(pattern)\n383\t\tif err != nil {\n384\t\t\treturn err\n385\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t// Calculate the expected range for this century\n388\t\tminCode := century * 100\n389\t\tmaxCode := minCode + 99\n390\t\n391\t\t// Validate the actual code falls within the range\n392\t\tif actual >= minCode && actual <= maxCode {\n393\t\t\treturn nil\n394\t\t}\n395\t\n396\t\t// Use the error formatting helper for enhanced range validation error messages\n397\t\t// This provides structured error information including:\n398\t\t// - Expected range (min/max)\n399\t\t// - Actual value received\n400\t\t// - Field name being validated\n401\t\t// - Contextual suggestions\n402\t\t// - Human-readable descriptions\n403\t\treturn validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern, actual, \"\", \"status_code\")\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t// parseStatusCodePattern parses a status code pattern like '4xx' and returns the century digit.\n407\t//\n408\t// This helper extracts the century digit from patterns in the format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5.\n409\t// It validates the pattern format and returns an error for invalid patterns.\n410\t//\n411\t// Parameters:\n412\t//   - pattern: The status code pattern to parse (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n413\t//\n414\t// Returns:\n415\t//   - The century digit (1-5) if the pattern is valid\n416\t//   - error if the pattern is invalid\n417\t//\n418\t// Example:\n419\t//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('4xx')\n420\t//   // century == 4, err == nil\n421\t//\n422\t// Example (invalid pattern):\n423\t//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('invalid')\n424\t//   // century == 0, err != nil\n425\tfunc parseStatusCodePattern(pattern string) (int, error) {\n426\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n427\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n428\t\t}\n429\t\n430\t\t// Check if pattern ends with 'xx'\n431\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n432\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\t// Extract the century digit\n436\t\tcentury := int(pattern[0] - '0')\n437\t\n438\t\t// Validate century is in valid HTTP range (1-5)\n439\t\tif century < 1 || century > 5 {\n440\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': century digit must be 1-5\", pattern)\n441\t\t}\n442\t\n443\t\treturn century, nil\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n447\t//\n448\t// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n449\t// - Expected range (min/max)\n478\t\t\t// - Actual value received\n479\t\t\t// - Field name being validated\n480\t\t\t// - Contextual suggestions\n481\t\t\t// - Human-readable descriptions\n482\t\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n483\t\t\tcontext := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation\", responseContext)\n484\t\t\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minCode, maxCode, actualCode, context, \"status_code\")\n485\t\n486\t\t\t// Output the enhanced error message\n487\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n%s\", err.Error())\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n492\t//\n493\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n494\t// in tests. interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n511\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n512\t\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n516\t//\n517\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n518\t// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n519\t//\n520\t// Parameters:\n521\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n522\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n523\t//\n524\t// server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/validate\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HTTP STATUS CODE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// This module provides comprehensive HTTP status code validation utilities\n// for error response testing. It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n// status codes, pattern-based range validation, and flexible validation patterns.\n//\n// Functions:\n// - ValidateStatusCodeInt: Basic status code validation with expected/actual ints\n// - ValidateStatusCodePattern: Validate status code matches pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code (test helper)\n// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n// - AssertStatusCode: Flexible validation supporting both assertion and boolean modes\n// - AssertStatusCodeAny: Flexible multi-code validation with assertion mode\n// - StatusCodeMatchResult: Detailed validation result with error context\n// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n// - parseStatusCodePattern: Parse pattern string to extract century digit\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeInt validates expected vs actual HTTP status codes.\n//\n// This is a basic status code validation function that compares two integers\n// and returns an error if they don't match. It's useful for simple validation\n// scenarios where you have direct access to status code integers.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the status codes match\n//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n//\n// Example (matching codes):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 200)\n//   // err == nil\n//\n// Example (non-matching codes):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 404)\n//   // err.Error() == \"status code mismatch: expected 200 (OK), got 404 (Not Found)\"\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeInt(expected, actual int) error {\n\tif expected == actual {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status code mismatch: expected %d (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n\t\texpected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected),\n\t\tactual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual))\n}\n\n// StatusCodeMatchResult contains detailed information about a status code validation.\n//\n// This type provides comprehensive error context for debugging validation failures,\n// including the expected and actual status codes with their descriptions, the\n// response object context, and a formatted error message.\ntype StatusCodeMatchResult struct {\n\t// Match indicates whether the status code matched successfully\n\tMatch bool\n\n\t// Expected is the status code that was expected\n\tExpected int\n\n\t// Actual is the status code that was received\n\tActual int\n\n\t// ExpectedDescription is the human-readable description of the expected status code\n\tExpectedDescription string\n\n\t// ActualDescription is the human-readable description of the actual status code\n\tActualDescription string\n\n\t// ResponseContext contains information about the response object\n\tResponseContext string\n\n\t// Error is a formatted error message describing the mismatch\n\tError string\n}\n\n// String returns a human-readable representation of the validation result.\nfunc (r StatusCodeMatchResult) String() string {\n\tif r.Match {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code match: %d (%s)\", r.Actual, r.ActualDescription)\n\t}\n\treturn r.Error\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCode validates status code with flexible assertion mode.\n//\n// This function supports both boolean-only mode (for conditional logic) and\n// assertion mode (with descriptive error messages). When assertMode is true\n// and validation fails, it returns a detailed error message showing expected\n// vs actual values along with response context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure; if false, return bool only\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n//\n// Example (boolean mode):\n//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 200, false)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       // Handle non-200 case\n//   }\n//\n// Example (assertion mode):\n//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 404, true)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       t.Error(result.Error)  // Full error with expected vs actual\n//   }\nfunc AssertStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\n\t// Build response context for error messages\n\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\n\t// Check if status codes match\n\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n\n\tif matches {\n\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build detailed error message\n\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchError(\n\t\texpectedCode,\n\t\tactualCode,\n\t\tresponseContext,\n\t)\n\n\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertStatusCodeAny validates status code against multiple allowed codes.\n//\n// This function is similar to AssertStatusCode but accepts multiple allowed\n// status codes. Returns detailed error information when validation fails,\n// including the full list of allowed status codes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable status codes\n//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n//\n// Example:\n//   result := AssertStatusCodeAny(w, []int{200, 201, 204}, true)\n//   if !result.Match {\n//       t.Error(result.Error)\n//   }\nfunc AssertStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\t\tExpected:           0,\n\t\t\tActual:             0,\n\t\t\tExpectedDescription: \"\",\n\t\t\tActualDescription:   \"\",\n\t\t\tResponseContext:     getStatusCodeResponseContext(response),\n\t\t\tError:              \"AssertStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\n\t// Check if actual code matches any in the allowed list\n\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n\t\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n\t\t\t\tExpected:           actualCode,\n\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build helpful error message showing what was allowed vs. what was received\n\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(\n\t\tallowedDesc,\n\t\tactualCode,\n\t\tresponseContext,\n\t)\n\n\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n\t\tMatch:              false,\n\t\tExpected:           0,\n\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n\t}\n}\n\n// buildStatusCodeMismatchError creates a detailed error message for status code mismatches.\n//\n// This helper builds a comprehensive error message that includes:\n// - The expected status code with description\n// - The actual status code with description\n// - Response object context (type and status if available)\nfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", expected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected)))\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n\n\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n//\n// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when validating against multiple\n// allowed status codes, showing all allowed codes vs the actual received code.\nfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedList string, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: one of [%s]\\n\", allowedList))\n\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n\n\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n//\n// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\nfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n\tswitch response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n\t}\n}\n\n// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n//\n// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n// including their descriptions for better debugging.\nfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor _, code := range codes {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n//\n// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n\n\tif !result.Match {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodePattern validates that an HTTP status code matches a pattern.\n//\n// This function validates status codes using pattern strings like '4xx', '5xx', etc.\n// It extracts the century digit from the pattern and validates that the actual code\n// falls within the corresponding range (e.g., '4xx' validates codes 400-499).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The status code pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx')\n//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the status code matches the pattern\n//   - error with descriptive message if pattern is invalid or code doesn't match\n//\n// Example (matching pattern):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 404)\n//   // err == nil\n//\n// Example (non-matching pattern):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 200)\n//   // err.Error() == \"status code 200 (OK) does not match pattern '4xx' (expected 400-499)\"\n//\n// Example (invalid pattern):\n//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('invalid', 200)\n//   // err.Error() == \"invalid status code pattern 'invalid': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\"\nfunc ValidateStatusCodePattern(pattern string, actual int) error {\n\t// Parse the pattern to extract the century digit\n\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate the expected range for this century\n\tminCode := century * 100\n\tmaxCode := minCode + 99\n\n\t// Validate the actual code falls within the range\n\tif actual >= minCode && actual <= maxCode {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the error formatting helper for enhanced range validation error messages\n\t// This provides structured error information including:\n\t// - Expected range (min/max)\n\t// - Actual value received\n\t// - Field name being validated\n\t// - Contextual suggestions\n\t// - Human-readable descriptions\n\treturn validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern, actual, \"\", \"status_code\")\n}\n\n// parseStatusCodePattern parses a status code pattern like '4xx' and returns the century digit.\n//\n// This helper extracts the century digit from patterns in the format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5.\n// It validates the pattern format and returns an error for invalid patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The status code pattern to parse (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - The century digit (1-5) if the pattern is valid\n//   - error if the pattern is invalid\n//\n// Example:\n//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('4xx')\n//   // century == 4, err == nil\n//\n// Example (invalid pattern):\n//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('invalid')\n//   // century == 0, err != nil\nfunc parseStatusCodePattern(pattern string) (int, error) {\n\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if pattern ends with 'xx'\n\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract the century digit\n\tcentury := int(pattern[0] - '0')\n\n\t// Validate century is in valid HTTP range (1-5)\n\tif century < 1 || century > 5 {\n\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': century digit must be 1-5\", pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn century, nil\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n//\n// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n// - Expected range (min/max)\n\t\t// - Actual value received\n\t\t// - Field name being validated\n\t\t// - Contextual suggestions\n\t\t// - Human-readable descriptions\n\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n\t\tcontext := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation\", responseContext)\n\t\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minCode, maxCode, actualCode, context, \"status_code\")\n\n\t\t// Output the enhanced error message\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n%s\", err.Error())\n\t}\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n// in tests. actualCode == expectedCode\n}\n\n// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n//\n// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code TestValidateStatusCodePattern_RangeInfoField tests that range validation errors\n// include the RangeInfo field with proper min/max values.\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodePattern_RangeInfoField(t \"500\", \"599\"},\n\t\t{\"2xx\", 500, \"200\", \"299\"},\n\t\t{\"3xx\", 200, \"300\", \"399\"},\n\t\t{\"1xx\", 404, \"100\", \"199\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.pattern, 500},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.pattern, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := ValidateStatusCodePattern(tt.pattern, tt.actual)\n\t\t\tif err == nil \"200\",\n\t\t\trangeInError:      \"400-499\",\n\t\t\tsuggestionsPresent: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:              \"5xx range with 404 should include all components\",\n\t\t\tminCode:           500,\n\t\t\tmaxCode:           599,\n\t\t\tactualCode:        404,\n\t\t\texpectedInError:   []string{\"500\", \"599\"},\n\t\t\tactualInError:     \"404\",\n\t\t\trangeInError: 399,\n\t\t\tactualCode:        200,\n\t\t\texpectedInError:   []string{\"300\", \"399\"},\n\t\t\tactualInError:     \"200\",\n\t\t\trangeInError:      \"300-399\",\n\t\t\tsuggestionsPresent: 404,\n\t\t\texpectedInError:   []string{\"100\", \"199\"},\n\t\t\tactualInError:     \"404\",\n\t\t\trangeInError:      \"100-199\",\n\t\t\tsuggestionsPresent: true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := contain actual value '%s', got: %s\", tt.actualInError, errorMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify range info is present\n\t\t\tif tt.rangeInError != \"\" && !strings.Contains(errorMsg, tt.rangeInError) \"status_code_range\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message should mention error type 'status_code_range', got: %s\", errorMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify suggestions section is present\n\t\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(line)\n\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"-\") || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"*\") {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Found a bullet point - should have content\n\t\t\t\t\tif len(trimmed) > 2 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\thasActionableContent = true\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif !hasActionableContent && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(errorMsg), \"suggestion\") TestValidateStatusCodeRange_HappyPath tests that valid ranges return no error.\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodeRange_HappyPath(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tminCode    int\n\t\tmaxCode    int\n\t\tactualCode int\n\t}{\n\t\t{200, 299, 200},\n\t\t{200, 299, 201},\n\t\t{200, 299, 299},\n\t\t{400, 499, 400},\n\t\t{400, 499, 404},\n\t\t{400, 499, 499},\n\t\t{500, 599, 500},\n\t\t{500, 599, 503},\n\t\t{500, 599, 599},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tname := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d_in_%d-%d\", tt.actualCode, tt.minCode, tt.maxCode)\n\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(tt.actualCode)\n\n\t\t\t// This should not produce any test failure\n\t\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, tt.minCode, tt.maxCode)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidateStatusCodeRange_InvalidMinMax tests that invalid min/max combinations are handled.\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodeRange_InvalidMinMax(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Skip this test if run in parallel with others since t.Fatalf will stop execution\n\tt.Skip(\"Testing Fatalf behavior requires subtest isolation\")\n\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tw.WriteHeader(200)\n\n\t// t.Fatalf will stop the test, so we can't actually test it in the same test\n\t// The behavior is tested implicitly by other tests using valid ranges\n\t_ = w\n\t_ = t\n\n\t// Note: ValidateStatusCodeRange calls t.Fatalf when min > max\n\t// This stops the test immediately, so we can't test it inline\n}\n\n// TestValidateStatusCodeRange_Integration tests the full ValidateStatusCodeRange\n// function with real testing.T to ensure it properly calls the error formatter.\n// Note: This test is expected to \"fail\" in the invalid range case because it\n// demonstrates that ValidateStatusCodeRange correctly calls t.Errorf when\n// validation fails. The error output shown is the enhanced error message.\nfunc TestValidateStatusCodeRange_Integration(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that invalid range produces test failure\n\tt.Run(\"invalid range produces error\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(404)\n\n\t\t// This should call t.Errorf with enhanced error message\n\t\t// Call the validation - it will fail the test, which is expected\n\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n\n\t\t// The function should have called t.Errorf with enhanced message\n\t\t// The test output above shows the enhanced error message format\n\t})\n\n\t// Test that valid range doesn't produce test failure\n\tt.Run(\"valid range produces no error\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(200)\n\n\t\t// This should not call t.Errorf\n\t\tValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n\t\t// Test should pass without any errors\n\t})\n}\n","numLines":807,"startLine":1,"totalLines":807}}}

=============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n481\t// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n482\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n486\t//\n487\t// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n488\t//\n489\t// Example usage:\n490\t//\n491\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n492\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n493\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n494\t//\n495\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n496\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n497\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\n498\tfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n499\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n500\t\t\treturn \"\"\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n504\t\n505\t\t// Format expected value\n506\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n507\t\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n508\t\t\tcase int:\n509\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n510\t\t\tcase []int:\n511\t\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n512\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n513\t\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n514\t\t\t\t}\n515\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n516\t\t\tcase string:\n517\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n518\t\t\tcase float64:\n519\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n520\t\t\tcase []string:\n521\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n522\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n523\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n526\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n527\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n528\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n529\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n530\t\t\tdefault:\n531\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Format actual value\n536\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n537\t\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n538\t\t\tcase int:\n539\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n540\t\t\tcase string:\n541\t\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n542\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n543\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n544\t\t\t\t}\n545\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n546\t\t\tcase float64:\n547\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n548\t\t\tcase []string:\n549\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n550\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n551\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n552\t\t\t\t}\n553\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n554\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n555\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n556\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n557\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n558\t\t\tdefault:\n559\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\t// Build the formatted string\n564\t\tvar parts []string\n565\t\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n566\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n567\t\t}\n568\t\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n569\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n573\t\t\treturn \"\"\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\n580\tfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n581\t\tif len(m) == 0 {\n582\t\t\treturn \"{}\"\n583\t\t}\n584\t\n585\t\tvar pairs []string\n586\t\tfor k, v := range m {\n587\t\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Limit output for large maps\n591\t\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n592\t\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n593\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\n600\tfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n601\t\tif len(s) == 0 {\n602\t\t\treturn \"[]\"\n603\t\t}\n604\t\n605\t\tvar items []string\n606\t\tfor _, v := range s {\n607\t\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\t// Limit output for large slices\n611\t\tif len(items) > 5 {\n612\t\t\titems = items[:5]\n613\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n620\t// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n621\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n622\t//\n623\t// Parameters:\n624\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n625\t//\n626\t// Returns a compact formatted string.\n627\t//\n628\t// Example usage:\n629\t//\n630\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n631\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\n633\tfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n634\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n635\t\t\treturn \"\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n643\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n647\t\t\treturn \"\"\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n658\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n659\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n660\t//\n661\t// Parameters:\n662\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n663\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n664\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n665\t//\n666\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n667\t//\n668\t// Example usage:\n669\t//\n670\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n671\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n672\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n673\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n674\t//\t}\n675\t//\n676\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n677\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n678\t//\n679\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n680\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n681\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n682\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n683\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n684\t\n685\t\t// Add severity if requested\n686\t\tif includeSeverity {\n687\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n688\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n689\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n690\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n691\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n692\t\t\t} else {\n693\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n694\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n695\t\t\t}\n696\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Add error type and field\n700\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n701\t\n702\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n703\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n704\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n705\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n706\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n709\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n710\t\t\t}\n711\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n712\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n713\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n715\t\t}\n716\t\n717\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n718\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n719\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n723\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n724\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n725\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n726\t\n727\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n728\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n732\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n733\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n734\t\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n737\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n741\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n742\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n743\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n744\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n745\t\t\t}\n746\t\t}\n747\t\n748\t\treturn builder.String()\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n752\t// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n753\t// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n754\t//\n755\t// Parameters:\n756\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n757\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n758\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n759\t//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n760\t//\n761\t// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n762\t//\n763\t// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n764\t// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n765\t// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n766\t//\n767\t// Example usage:\n768\t//\n769\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n770\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n771\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n772\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n773\t//\t}\n774\t//\n775\t//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n776\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n777\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n778\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n779\t//\n780\t//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n781\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n782\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n783\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n784\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n785\t\n786\t\t// Add severity if requested\n787\t\tif includeSeverity {\n788\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n789\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n790\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n791\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n792\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n793\t\t\t} else {\n794\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n795\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n796\t\t\t}\n797\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n798\t\t}\n799\t\n800\t\t// Add error type and field\n801\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n802\t\n803\t\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n804\t\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n805\t\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n806\t\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n807\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n808\t\t\t}\n809\t\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n810\t\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n811\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n812\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n813\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n814\t\t\t}\n815\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n816\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n817\t\t\t}\n818\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n819\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n820\t\t\t}\n821\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n822\t\t}\n823\t\n824\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n825\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n826\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n827\t\t}\n828\t\n829\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n830\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n831\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n832\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n833\t\n834\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n835\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n836\t\t\t}\n837\t\n838\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n839\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n840\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n841\t\t\t}\n842\t\n843\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n844\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n845\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n848\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n849\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n850\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n851\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n852\t\t\t}\n853\t\t}\n854\t\n855\t\treturn builder.String()\n856\t}\n857\t\n858\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n859\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n860\t//\n861\t// Parameters:\n862\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n863\t//\n864\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n865\t//\n866\t// Example usage:\n867\t//\n868\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n869\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n870\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n871\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n872\t//\t}\n873\t//\n874\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n875\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n876\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n877\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n878\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n879\t\t}\n880\t\n881\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n882\t}\n883\t\n884\t// =============================================================================\n885\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n886\t// =============================================================================\n887\t\n888\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n889\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n890\t//\n891\t// Parameters:\n892\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n893\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n894\t//\n895\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n896\t//\n897\t// Example usage:\n898\t//\n899\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n900\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n901\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n902\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n903\t//\t}, {\n904\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n905\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n906\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n907\t//\t}}\n908\t//\n909\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n910\t//\t// Returns:\n911\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n912\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n913\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n914\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n915\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n916\t\t}\n917\t\n918\t\tvar lines []string\n919\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n920\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n921\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n922\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n923\t\t}\n924\t\n925\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n926\t}\n927\t\n928\t// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n929\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n930\t//\n931\t// Parameters:\n932\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n933\t//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n934\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n935\t//\n936\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n937\t//\n938\t// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n939\t//\n940\t// Example usage:\n941\t//\n942\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n943\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n944\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n945\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n946\t//\t}, {\n947\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n948\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n949\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n950\t//\t}}\n951\t//\n952\t//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n953\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n954\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n955\t//\t}\n956\t//\n957\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n958\t//\t// Returns:\n959\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n960\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\n961\tfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n962\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n963\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\tvar lines []string\n967\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n968\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n969\t\n970\t\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n971\t\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n972\t\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n973\t\t\t}\n974\t\n975\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n976\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n977\t\t}\n978\t\n979\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n980\t}\n981\t\n982\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n983\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n984\t//\n985\t// Parameters:\n986\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n987\t//\n988\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n989\t//\n990\t// Example usage:\n991\t//\n992\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n993\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n994\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n995\t//\t}, {\n996\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n997\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n998\t//\t}}\n999\t//\n1000\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n1001\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n1002\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n1003\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n1004\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n1005\t\t}\n1006\t\n1007\t\t// Count errors by type\n1008\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n1009\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n1010\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n1011\t\t}\n1012\t\n1013\t\t// Build summary parts\n1014\t\tvar parts []string\n1015\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n1016\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n1020\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t// =============================================================================\n1023\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n1024\t// =============================================================================\n1025\t\n1026\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n1027\t// without the parent parameter.\n1028\t//\n1029\t// Parameters:\n1030\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n1031\t//   - location: Additional location context\n1032\t//\n1033\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n1034\t//\n1035\t// Example usage:\n1036\t//\n1037\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n1038\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n1039\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n1040\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n1041\t}\n1042\t\n1043\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n1044\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n1045\t//\n1046\t// Parameters:\n1047\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n1048\t//\n1049\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n1050\t//\n1051\t// Example usage:\n1052\t//\n1053\t//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n1054\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n1055\tfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n1056\t\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n1057\t}\n1058\t\n1059\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n1060\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n1061\t//\n1062\t// Parameters:\n1063\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n1064\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n1065\t//\n1066\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n1067\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n1068\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n1069\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n1072\t// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n1073\t// of formatting an error with context.\n1074\t//\n1075\t// Parameters:\n1076\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n1077\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n1078\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n1079\t//\n1080\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n1081\t//\n1082\t// Example usage:\n1083\t//\n1084\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n1085\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n1086\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n1087\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n1088\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n1089\t}\n1090\t\n1091\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n1092\t//\n1093\t// Parameters:\n1094\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n1095\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n1096\t//\n1097\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n1098\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n1099\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n1100\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t// =============================================================================\n1103\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1104\t// =============================================================================\n1105\t\n1106\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n1107\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n1108\t//\n1109\t// Parameters:\n1110\t//   - value: The value to quote\n1111\t//\n1112\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n1113\t//\n1114\t// Example usage:\n1115\t//\n1116\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n1117\t//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n1118\t//\n1119\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n1120\t//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\n1121\tfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n1122\t\tif value == \"\" {\n1123\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n1124\t\t}\n1125\t\n1126\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n1127\t}\n1128\t\n1129\t// TruncateString
