validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"💡\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"❓\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n//\n// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\nfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n\n\t// Format expected value\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n\t\tcase []string:\n\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n\t\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Format actual value\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n\t\tcase []string:\n\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n\t\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the formatted string\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n\t}\n\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\nfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n\tif len(m) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"{}\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar pairs []string\n\tfor k, v := range m {\n\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit output for large maps\n\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n}\n\n// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\nfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n\tif len(s) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"[]\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar items []string\n\tfor _, v := range s {\n\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit output for large slices\n\tif len(items) > 5 {\n\t\titems = items[:5]\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n}\n\n// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n//\n// Returns a compact formatted string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\nfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n\t}\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add suggestions if present\n\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n//\n// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n//\n//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n\t\t}\n\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add suggestions if present\n\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\nfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\n\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n// of formatting an error with context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n","numLines":1154,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1154}}}

All other fields are optional.\n25\t// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n26\t// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n27\t//\n28\t// # Common Usage Pattern\n29\t//\n30\t// The typical workflow is:\n31\t//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n32\t//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n33\t//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n34\t//\n35\t// # Example\n36\t//\n37\t//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n38\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n39\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n40\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n41\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n42\t//\t    Format()\n43\t//\n44\t//\t// With custom suggestions\n45\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n46\t//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n47\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n48\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n49\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n50\t//\t    Format()\n51\t//\n52\t// # Extending for New Validation Types\n53\t//\n54\t// To add support for a new validation type:\n55\t//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n56\t//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n57\t//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n58\t//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n59\t//\n60\t// # Fields\n61\t//\n62\t// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n63\t// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\n64\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n65\t\tvalidationType string\n66\t\texpected       interface{}\n67\t\tactual         interface{}\n68\t\tcontext        string\n69\t\tresponseSnippet string\n70\t\tfieldName      string\n71\t\tpatternDetails string\n72\t\trangeInfo      string\n73\t\tvalidationDetails []string\n74\t\tcustomSuggestions []string\n75\t}\n76\t\n77\t// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n78\t//\n79\t// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n80\t// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n81\t//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n82\t//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n83\t//   - Provide context for error handling\n84\t//\n85\t// # Parameters\n86\t//\n87\t//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n88\t//\n89\t//     Common validation types include:\n90\t//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n91\t//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n92\t//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n93\t//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n94\t//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n95\t//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n96\t//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n97\t//\n98\t// # Returns\n99\t//\n100\t// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n101\t//\n102\t// # Example Usage\n103\t//\n104\t//\t// Basic status code validation\n105\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n106\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n107\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n108\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n109\t//\t    Format()\n110\t//\n111\t//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n112\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n113\t//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n114\t//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n115\t//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n116\t//\t    Format()\n117\t//\n118\t//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n119\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n120\t//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n121\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n122\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n123\t//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n124\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n125\t//\t    Format()\n126\tfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n127\t\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n128\t\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n129\t\t}\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n133\t//\n134\t// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n135\t// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n136\t// error messages showing what went wrong.\n137\t//\n138\t// # Parameters\n139\t//\n140\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n141\t//\n142\t//     Common types:\n143\t//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n144\t//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n145\t//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n146\t//\n147\t// # Returns\n148\t//\n149\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n150\t//\n151\t// # Example\n152\t//\n153\t//\t// Single expected value\n154\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n155\t//\n156\t//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n157\t//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n158\t//\n159\t//\t// String pattern\n160\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\n161\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n162\t\tvf.expected = expected\n163\t\treturn vf\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n167\t//\n168\t// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n169\t// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n170\t// that caused the validation failure.\n171\t//\n172\t// # Parameters\n173\t//\n174\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n175\t//\n176\t//     Common types:\n177\t//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n178\t//     - string: Actual message or content received\n179\t//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n180\t//\n181\t// # Returns\n182\t//\n183\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n184\t//\n185\t// # Example\n186\t//\n187\t//\t// Status code that was received\n188\t//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n189\t//\n190\t//\t// Error message that was received\n191\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n192\t//\n193\t//\t// Content-Type that was received\n194\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\n195\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n196\t\tvf.actual = actual\n197\t\treturn vf\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\t// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n201\t//\n202\t// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n203\t// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n204\t// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n205\t//\n206\t// # Parameters\n207\t//\n208\t//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n209\t//\n210\t//     Common context examples:\n211\t//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n212\t//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n213\t//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n214\t//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n215\t//\n216\t// # Returns\n217\t//\n218\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n219\t//\n220\t// # Example\n221\t//\n222\t//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n223\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n224\t//\n225\t//\t// Operation context\n226\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n227\t//\n228\t//\t// Test scenario context\n229\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\n230\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n231\t\tvf.context = context\n232\t\treturn vf\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n236\t//\n237\t// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n238\t// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n239\t// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n240\t//\n241\t// # Parameters\n242\t//\n243\t//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n244\t//\n245\t//     Best practices:\n246\t//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n247\t//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n248\t//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n249\t//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n250\t//\n251\t// # Returns\n252\t//\n253\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n254\t//\n255\t// # Example\n256\t//\n257\t//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n258\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n259\t//\n260\t//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n261\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n262\t//\n263\t//\t// Error message snippet\n264\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\n265\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n266\t\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n267\t\treturn vf\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n271\t//\n272\t// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n273\t// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n274\t// configuration objects.\n275\t//\n276\t// # Parameters\n277\t//\n278\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n279\t//\n280\t//     Common examples:\n281\t//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n282\t//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n283\t//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n284\t//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n285\t//\n286\t// # Returns\n287\t//\n288\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n289\t//\n290\t// # Example\n291\t//\n292\t//\t// Simple field name\n293\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n294\t//\n295\t//\t// Nested field notation\n296\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n297\t//\n298\t//\t// Array field notation\n299\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\n300\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n301\t\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n302\t\treturn vf\n303\t}\n304\t\n305\t// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n306\t//\n307\t// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n308\t// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n309\t// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n310\t//\n311\t// # Parameters\n312\t//\n313\t//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n314\t//\n315\t//     Common examples:\n316\t//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n317\t//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n318\t//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n319\t//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n320\t//\n321\t// # When to Use\n322\t//\n323\t// Use this method when:\n324\t//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n325\t//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n326\t//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n327\t//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n328\t//\n329\t// # Returns\n330\t//\n331\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n332\t//\n333\t// # Example\n334\t//\n335\t//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n336\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n337\t//\n338\t//\t// Format requirement\n339\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n340\t//\n341\t//\t// Content pattern description\n342\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\n343\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n344\t\tvf.patternDetails = details\n345\t\treturn vf\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n349\t//\n350\t// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n351\t// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n352\t// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n353\t//\n354\t// # Parameters\n355\t//\n356\t//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n357\t//\n358\t//     Common examples:\n359\t//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n360\t//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n361\t//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n362\t//\n363\t// # When to Use\n364\t//\n365\t// Use this method when:\n366\t//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n367\t//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n368\t//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n369\t//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n370\t//\n371\t// # Returns\n372\t//\n373\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n374\t//\n375\t// # Example\n376\t//\n377\t//\t// Status code range\n378\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n379\t//\n380\t//\t// Numeric range\n381\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n382\t//\n383\t//\t// Descriptive range\n384\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\n385\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n386\t\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n387\t\treturn vf\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n391\t//\n392\t// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n393\t// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n394\t// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n395\t//\n396\t// # Parameters\n397\t//\n398\t//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n399\t//\n400\t//     Common uses:\n401\t//     - List specific conditions that failed\n402\t//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n403\t//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n404\t//     - Document intermediate validation states\n405\t//\n406\t// # When to Use\n407\t//\n408\t// Use this method when:\n409\t//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n410\t//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n411\t//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n412\t//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n413\t//\n414\t// # Returns\n415\t//\n416\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n417\t//\n418\t// # Example\n419\t//\n420\t//\t// Multiple specific failures\n421\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n422\t//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n423\t//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n424\t//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n425\t//\t)\n426\t//\n427\t//\t// Detailed explanation\n428\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n429\t//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n430\t//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n431\t//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n432\t//\t)\n433\t//\n434\t//\t// Step-by-step validation\n435\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n436\t//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n437\t//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n438\t//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n439\t//\t)\n440\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n441\t\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n442\t\treturn vf\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n446\t//\n447\t// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n448\t// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n449\t// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n450\t//\n451\t// # Parameters\n452\t//\n453\t//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n454\t//\n455\t//     Best practices for suggestions:\n456\t//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n457\t//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n458\t//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n459\t//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n460\t//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n461\t//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n462\t//\n463\t// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n464\t//\n465\t// Use this method when:\n466\t//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n467\t//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n468\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n469\t//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n470\t//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n471\t//\n472\t// # Returns\n473\t//\n474\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n475\t//\n476\t// # Example\n477\t//\n478\t//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n479\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n480\t//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n481\t//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n482\t//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n483\t//\t)\n484\t//\n485\t//\t// Specific debugging steps\n486\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n487\t//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n488\t//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n489\t//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n490\t//\t)\n491\t//\n492\t//\t// Reference documentation\n493\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n494\t//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n495\t//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n496\t//\t)\n497\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n498\t\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n499\t\treturn vf\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n503\t//\n504\t// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n505\t// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n506\t// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n507\t//\n508\t// # Behavior\n509\t//\n510\t// The Format() method:\n511\t//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n512\t//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n513\t//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n514\t//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n515\t//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n516\t//\n517\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n518\t//\n519\t// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n520\t//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n521\t//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n522\t//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n523\t//\n524\t// # Returns\n525\t//\n526\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n527\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n528\t// test assertions.\n529\t//\n530\t// # Example\n531\t//\n532\t//\t// Complete usage example\n533\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n534\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n535\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n536\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n537\t//\t    Format()\n538\t//\n539\t//\t// Error() method will produce:\n540\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n541\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n542\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n543\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n544\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n545\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n546\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n547\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n548\t//\n549\t//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n550\t//\treturn err\n551\t//\n552\t//\t// Or formatted for logging\n553\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n554\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n555\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n556\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n557\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n558\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n559\t\t}\n560\t\n561\t\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n562\t\treturn ValidationError{\n563\t\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n564\t\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n565\t\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n566\t\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n567\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n568\t\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n569\t\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n570\t\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n571\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n572\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n573\t\t}\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\t// =============================================================================\n577\t// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n578\t// =============================================================================\n579\t//\n580\t// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n581\t// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n582\t//\n583\t// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n584\t//\n585\t// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n586\t//\n587\t// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n588\t//\n589\t// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n590\t//\n591\t//\t// Custom header validation\n592\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n593\t//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n594\t//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n595\t//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n596\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n597\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n598\t//\t    Format()\n599\t//\n600\t// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n601\t//\n602\t// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n603\t// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n604\t//\n605\t//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n606\t//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n607\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n608\t//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n609\t//\t        WithActual(actual).\n610\t//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n611\t//\t        WithContext(context).\n612\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n613\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n614\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n615\t//\t        ).\n616\t//\t        Format()\n617\t//\t}\n618\t//\n619\t// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n620\t//\n621\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n622\t//\n623\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n624\t//\t    \"response_time\",\n625\t//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n626\t//\t    \"250ms\",\n627\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n628\t//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n629\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n630\t//\t)\n631\t//\n632\t// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n633\t//\n634\t// When creating custom validation types:\n635\t//\n636\t// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n637\t//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n638\t//\n639\t// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n640\t//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n641\t//\n642\t// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n643\t//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n644\t//\n645\t// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n646\t//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n647\t//\n648\t// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n649\t//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n650\t//\n651\t// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n652\t//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n653\t//\n654\t// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n655\t//\n656\t// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n657\t// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n658\t// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n659\t// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n660\t//\n661\t// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n662\t//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n663\t//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n664\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n665\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n666\t//\n667\t// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n668\t//\n669\t// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n670\t//\n671\t//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n672\t//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n673\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n674\t//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n675\t//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n676\t//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n677\t//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n678\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n679\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n680\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n681\t//\t        ).\n682\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n683\t//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n684\t//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n685\t//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n686\t//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n687\t//\t        ).\n688\t//\t        Format()\n689\t//\t}\n690\t//\n691\t//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n692\t//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n693\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n694\t//\n695\t// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n696\t// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n697\t\n698\t// =============================================================================\n699\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n700\t// =============================================================================\n701\t\n702\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n703\t//\n704\t// Parameters:\n705\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n706\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n707\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n708\t//\n709\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n710\t//\n711\t// Example usage:\n712\t//\n713\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n714\t//\t// Output:\n715\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n716\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n717\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n718\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n719\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n720\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n721\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n722\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n723\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n724\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n725\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n726\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n727\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n728\t\t\tFormat()\n729\t}\n730\t\n731\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n732\t//\n733\t// Parameters:\n734\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n735\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n736\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n737\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n738\t//\n739\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n740\t//\n741\t// Example usage:\n742\t//\n743\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n744\t//\t// Output:\n745\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n746\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n747\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n748\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n749\t//\t//   Field:    error\n750\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n751\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n752\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n753\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n754\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n755\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n756\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n757\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n758\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n759\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n760\t\t\tFormat()\n761\t}\n762\t\n763\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n764\t//\n765\t// Parameters:\n766\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n767\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n768\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n769\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n770\t//\n771\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n772\t//\n773\t// Example usage:\n774\t//\n775\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\", \"status_code\")\n776\t//\t// Output:\n777\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n778\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n779\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n780\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n781\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n782\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n783\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n784\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n785\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n786\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n787\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n788\t\n789\t\t// Build range info string\n790\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n791\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n792\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n793\t\t}\n794\t\n795\t\tdetails := []string{}\n796\t\tif err != nil {\n797\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n798\t\t} else {\n799\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n800\t\t}\n801\t\n802\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n803\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n804\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n805\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n806\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n807\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n808\t\n809\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n810\t\tif err == nil {\n811\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n812\t\t}\n813\t\n814\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n815\t}\n816\t\n817\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n818\t//\n819\t// Parameters:\n820\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n821\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n822\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n823\t//\n824\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n825\t//\n826\t// Example usage:\n827\t//\n828\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n829\t//\t// Output:\n830\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n831\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n832\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n833\t//   Context:  API response\n834\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n835\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n836\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n837\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n838\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n839\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n840\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n841\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n842\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n843\t\t\tFormat()\n844\t}\n845\t\n846\t// FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError creates a validation error for min/max range validation failures.\n847\t//\n848\t// This function is used when validating status codes (or other numeric values) against\n849\t// explicit minimum and maximum boundaries. It provides enhanced error messages showing\n850\t// the exact range that was expected and the actual value that was received.\n851\t//\n852\t// Parameters:\n853\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value (inclusive)\n854\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value (inclusive)\n855\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that was received\n856\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation (e.g., \"status code validation\")\n857\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n858\t//\n859\t// Returns a ValidationError with range information and appropriate suggestions.\n860\t//\n861\t// Example usage:\n862\t//\n863\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(200, 299, 404, \"success response check\", \"status_code\")\n864\t//\t// Output:\n865\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n866\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n867\t//\t//   Expected: 200-299 (Success)\n868\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n869\t//\t//   Range:    200-299 (Success)\n870\t//\t//   Context:  success response check\n871\t//\t//   Validation Details:\n872\t//\t//     - Status code 404 is outside valid range 200-299\n873\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n874\t//\t//     - Check if the endpoint URL is correct\n875\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n876\t//\t//     - Ensure authentication credentials are valid\n877\tfunc FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minValue, maxValue, actualValue int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n878\t\t// Get range description\n879\t\tdesc := getRangeDescriptionForValues(minValue, maxValue)\n880\t\n881\t\t// Build range info string\n882\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)\n883\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n884\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", minValue, maxValue, desc)\n885\t\t}\n886\t\n887\t\tdetails := []string{\n888\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside valid range %d-%d\", actualValue, minValue, maxValue),\n889\t\t}\n890\t\n891\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n892\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)).\n893\t\t\tWithActual(actualValue).\n894\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n895\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n896\t\t\tWithRangeInfo(rangeInfo).\n897\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...).\n898\t\t\tFormat()\n899\t}\n900\t\n901\t// getRangeDescriptionForValues returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n902\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges.\n903\tfunc getRangeDescriptionForValues(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n904\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n905\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n906\t\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n907\t\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n908\t\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n909\t\t\t\"100-199\": \"Informational\",\n910\t\t}\n911\t\n912\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n913\t\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n914\t\t\treturn desc\n915\t\t}\n916\t\n917\t\treturn \"\"\n918\t\n919\t}\n920\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n921\t//\n922\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n923\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n924\t// your specific requirements.\n925\t//\n926\t// Parameters:\n927\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n928\t//   - expected: The expected value\n929\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n930\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n931\t//\n932\t// Example usage:\n933\t//\n934\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n935\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n936\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n937\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n938\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n939\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n940\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n941\t//\t)\n942\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n943\t\tvalidationType string,\n944\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n945\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n946\t) ValidationError {\n947\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n948\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n949\t\t\topt(config)\n950\t\t}\n951\t\n952\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n953\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n954\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n955\t\n956\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n957\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n958\t\t}\n959\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n960\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n961\t\t}\n962\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n963\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n964\t\t}\n965\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n966\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n967\t\t}\n968\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n969\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n970\t\t}\n971\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n972\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n973\t\t}\n974\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n975\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n976\t\t}\n977\t\n978\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n979\t}\n980\t\n981\t// =============================================================================\n982\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n983\t// =============================================================================\n984\t\n985\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n986\ttype FormatConfig struct []string\n993\t\tSuggestions        []string\n994\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n995\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n996\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n997\t\tStatusCode         int\n998\t\tTimeout            int\n999\t\tSecurityContext    string\n1000\t}\n1001\t\n1002\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n1003\ttype FormatOption {\n1008\t\t\tc.Context = context\n1009\t\t}\n1010\t}\n1011\t\n1012\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n1013\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string)
