FUNCTIONS\n1142\t// =============================================================================\n1143\t\n1144\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1145\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1146\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1147\t\tif err != nil {\n1148\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1149\t\t}\n1150\t\n1151\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1152\t\tif err != nil {\n1153\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1154\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1155\t\t}\n1156\t\n1157\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1158\t}\n1159\t\n1160\t// =============================================================================\n1161\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1162\t// =============================================================================\n1163\t\n1164\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1165\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1166\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1167\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1168\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1169\t}\n1170\t\n1171\t// global tracker instance\n1172\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1173\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1174\t}\n1175\t\n1176\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1177\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1178\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1179\t//\n1180\t// Parameters:\n1181\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1182\t//\n1183\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1184\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1185\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1186\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1187\t\n1188\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1189\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1190\t}\n1191\t\n1192\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1193\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1194\t// configuration issues.\n1195\t//\n1196\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1197\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1198\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1199\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1200\t\n1201\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1202\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1203\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1204\t\t}\n1205\t\treturn result\n1206\t}\n1207\t\n1208\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1209\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1210\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1211\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1212\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1213\t\n1214\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1215\t}\n1216\t\n1217\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1218\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1219\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1220\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1221\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1222\t\n1223\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1224\t}\n1225\t\n1226\t// =============================================================================\n1227\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1228\t// =============================================================================\n1229\t\n1230\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1231\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1232\t//\n1233\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1234\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1235\t//\n1236\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1237\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1238\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1239\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1240\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1241\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1242\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1243\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1244\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1245\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1246\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1247\t//\n1248\t// Parameters:\n1249\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1250\t//   - message: The error message content\n1251\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1252\t//\n1253\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1254\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1255\t//\n1256\t// Example usage:\n1257\t//\n1258\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1259\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1260\t//\n1261\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1262\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1263\t//\n1264\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1265\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1266\t//\n1267\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1268\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1269\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1270\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1271\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1272\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1273\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1274\t\n1275\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1276\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1277\t\n1278\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1279\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1280\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1281\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1282\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1283\t\t\t} else {\n1284\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1285\t\t\t}\n1286\t\t}\n1287\t\n1288\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1289\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1290\t}\n1291\t\n1292\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1293\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1294\t//\n1295\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1296\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1297\t//\n1298\t// Error Type Validation:\n1299\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1300\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1301\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1302\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1303\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1304\t//\n1305\t// Parameters:\n1306\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1307\t//   - message: The error message content\n1308\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1309\t//\n1310\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1311\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1312\t//\n1313\t// Example usage:\n1314\t//\n1315\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1316\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1317\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1318\t//\n1319\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1320\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1321\t//\n1322\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1323\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1324\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1325\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1326\t//\n1327\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1328\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1329\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1330\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1331\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1332\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1333\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1334\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1335\t\t}\n1336\t\n1337\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1338\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1339\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1340\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1341\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1342\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1343\t\n1344\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1345\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1346\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1347\t\t}\n1348\t\n1349\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1350\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1351\t\n1352\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1353\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1354\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1355\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1356\t\n1357\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1358\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1359\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1360\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1361\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1362\t\t}\n1363\t\n1364\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1365\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1366\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1367\t\n1368\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1369\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1370\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1371\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1372\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1373\t\t\t} else {\n1374\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1375\t\t\t}\n1376\t\t}\n1377\t\n1378\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1379\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1380\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1381\t\t}\n1382\t\n1383\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1384\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1385\t}\n1386\t\n1387\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1388\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1389\t//\n1390\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1391\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1392\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1393\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1394\t}\n1395\t\n1396\t// =============================================================================\n1397\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1398\t// =============================================================================\n1399\t\n1400\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1401\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1402\t\n1403\tconst (\n1404\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1405\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1406\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1407\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1408\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1409\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1410\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1411\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1412\t)\n1413\t\n1414\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1415\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1416\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1417\t\tprefix     string\n1418\t}\n1419\t\n1420\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1421\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1422\t\n1423\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1424\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1425\t//\n1426\t// Example usage:\n1427\t//\n1428\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1429\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1430\t//\n1431\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1432\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1433\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1434\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1435\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1436\t\t}\n1437\t}\n1438\t\n1439\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1440\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1441\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1442\t//\n1443\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1444\t//\n1445\t// Example usage:\n1446\t//\n1447\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1448\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1449\t//\n1450\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1451\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1452\t//\n1453\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1454\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1455\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1456\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1457\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1458\t\t}\n1459\t}\n1460\t\n1461\t// =============================================================================\n1462\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1463\t// =============================================================================\n1464\t\n1465\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1466\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1467\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1468\t//\n1469\t// Parameters:\n1470\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1471\t//\n1472\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1473\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1474\t//\n1475\t// Example usage:\n1476\t//\n1477\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1478\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1479\t//\n1480\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1481\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1482\t//\n1483\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1484\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1485\t//\n1486\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1487\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1488\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1489\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1490\t\n1491\t\t// Handle empty path\n1492\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1493\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1494\t\t}\n1495\t\n1496\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1497\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1498\t}\n1499\t\n1500\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1501\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1502\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1503\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1504\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1505\t\t}\n1506\t\n1507\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1508\t\tvar result []string\n1509\t\n1510\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1511\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1512\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1513\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1514\t\t\t}\n1515\t\n1516\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1517\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1518\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1519\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1520\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1521\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1522\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1523\t\t\t\t} else {\n1524\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1525\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1526\t\t\t\t}\n1527\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1528\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1529\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1530\t\t\t} else {\n1531\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1532\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1533\t\t\t}\n1534\t\t}\n1535\t\n1536\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1537\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1538\t\t}\n1539\t\n1540\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1541\t}\n1542\t\n1543\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1544\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1545\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1546\t\t\treturn false\n1547\t\t}\n1548\t\n1549\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1550\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1551\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1552\t\t\t\treturn false\n1553\t\t\t}\n1554\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1555\t\t}\n1556\t\n1557\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1558\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1559\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1560\t\t\t\treturn false\n1561\t\t\t}\n1562\t\t}\n1563\t\n1564\t\treturn true\n1565\t}\n1566\t\n1567\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1568\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1569\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1570\t}\n1571\t\n1572\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1573\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1574\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1575\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1576\t//\n1577\t// Parameters:\n1578\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1579\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1580\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1581\t//\n1582\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1583\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1584\t//\n1585\t// Example usage:\n1586\t//\n1587\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1588\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1589\t//\n1590\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1591\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1592\t//\n1593\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1594\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1595\t//\n1596\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1597\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1598\t//\n1599\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1600\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1601\t//\n1602\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1603\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1604\t//\n1605\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1606\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1607\t//\n1608\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1609\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1610\t//\n1611\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1612\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1613\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1614\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1615\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1616\t\n1617\t\t// Apply options\n1618\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1619\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1620\t\t\topt(config)\n1621\t\t}\n1622\t\n1623\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1624\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1625\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1626\t\t}\n1627\t\n1628\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1629\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1630\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1631\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1632\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1633\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1634\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1635\t\t\t\t}\n1636\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1637\t\t\t}\n1638\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1639\t\t}\n1640\t\n1641\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1642\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1643\t\n1644\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1645\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1646\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1647\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1648\t\t\t}\n1649\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1650\t\t}\n1651\t\n1652\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1653\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1654\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1655\t\t}\n1656\t\n1657\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1658\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1659\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1660\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1661\t\n1662\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1663\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1664\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1665\t\t\t}\n1666\t\n1667\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1668\t\t}\n1669\t\n1670\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1671\t}\n1672\t\n1673\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1674\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1675\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1676\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1677\t\t\treturn path\n1678\t\t}\n1679\t\n1680\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1681\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1682\t\tinBrackets := false\n1683\t\n1684\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1685\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1686\t\n1687\t\t\tswitch {\n1688\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1689\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1690\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1691\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1692\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1693\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1694\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1695\t\t\t\t}\n1696\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1697\t\n1698\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1699\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1700\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1701\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1702\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1703\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1704\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1705\t\t\t\t}\n1706\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1707\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1708\t\n1709\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1710\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1711\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1712\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1713\t\n1714\t\t\tdefault:\n1715\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1716\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1717\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1718\t\t\t\t} else {\n1719\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1720\t\t\t\t}\n1721\t\t\t}\n1722\t\t}\n1723\t\n1724\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1725\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1726\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1727\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1728\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1729\t\t}\n1730\t\n1731\t\treturn result.String()\n1732\t}\n1733\t\n1734\t// =============================================================================\n1735\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1736\t// =============================================================================\n1737\t\n1738\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1739\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1740\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1741\t//\n1742\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1743\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1744\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1745\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1746\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1747\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1748\t//\n1749\t// Parameters:\n1750\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1751\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1752\t//   - message: The error message content\n1753\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1754\t//\n1755\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1756\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1757\t//\n1758\t// Example usage:\n1759\t//\n1760\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1761\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1762\t//\n1763\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1764\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1765\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1766\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1767\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1768\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1769\t\t}\n1770\t\n1771\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1772\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1773\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1774\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1775\t\n1776\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1777\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1778\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1779\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1780\t\t\t} else {\n1781\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1782\t\t\t}\n1783\t\t}\n1784\t\n1785\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1786\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1787\t\n1788\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1789\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1790\t\n1791\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1792\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1793\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1794\t\n1795\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1796\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1797\t\n1798\t\t// Add field name if present\n1799\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1800\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1801\t\t}\n1802\t\n1803\t\t// Add message\n1804\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1805\t\n1806\t\treturn builder.String()\n1807\t}\n1808\t\n1809\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1810\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1811\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1812\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1813\t\n1814\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1815\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1816\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1817\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1818\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1819\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1820\t\t}\n1821\t\n1822\t\treturn \"\"\n1823\t}\n1824\t\n1825\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1826\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1827\t\tswitch severity {\n1828\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1829\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1830\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1831\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1832\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1833\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1834\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1835\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1836\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1837\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1838\t\tdefault:\n1839\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1840\t\t}\n1841\t}\n1842\t\n1843\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1844\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1845\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1846\t//\n1847\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1848\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1849\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1850\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1851\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1852\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1853\t//\n1854\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1855\t//\n1856\t// Parameters:\n1857\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1858\t//   - message: The error message content\n1859\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1860\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1861\t//\n1862\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1863\t//\n1864\t// Example usage:\n1865\t//\n1866\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1867\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1868\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1869\t//\t    \"email\",\n1870\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1871\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1872\t//\t)\n1873\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1874\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1875\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1876\t\tmessage string,\n1877\t\tfieldName string,\n1878\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1879\t) string {\n1880\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1881\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1882\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1883\t\n1884\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1885\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1886\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1887\t\t\topt(config)\n1888\t\t}\n1889\t\n1890\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1891\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1892\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1893\t\t}\n1894\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1895\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1896\t\t}\n1897\t\n1898\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1899\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1900\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1901\t\n1902\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1903\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1904\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1905\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1906\t\t\t} else {\n1907\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1908\t\t\t}\n1909\t\t}\n1910\t\n1911\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1912\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1913\t\n1914\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n1915\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n1916\t\n1917\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1918\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1919\t\n1920\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1921\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1922\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1923\t\n1924\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n1925\t\tif category != CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n1926\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n1927\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1928\t\t}\n1929\t\n1930\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1931\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n1932\t\n1933\t\t// Add field name if present\n1934\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1935\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1936\t\t}\n1937\t\n1938\t\t// Add message\n1939\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1940\t\n1941\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1942\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1943\t\n1944\t\treturn builder.String()\n1945\t}\n1946\t\n1947\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1948\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1949\t\tswitch category {\n1950\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1951\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1952\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1953\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1954\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1955\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1956\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1957\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n1958\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1959\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n1960\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n1961\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n1962\t\tdefault:\n1963\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1964\t\t}\n1965\t}\n1966\t\n1967\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n1968\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n1969\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n1970\t\tif config == nil {\n1971\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1972\t\t}\n1973\t\n1974\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n1975\t\n1976\t\tswitch category {\n1977\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1978\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n1979\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n1980\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n1981\t\t\t}\n1982\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1983\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n1984\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n1985\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (timeout: %dms)\", config.Timeout))\n1986\t\t\t}\n1987\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1988\t\t\t// Add security-specific context\n1989\t\t\tif config.SecurityContext != \"\" {\n1990\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (security: %s)\", config.SecurityContext))\n1991\t\t\t}\n1992\t\t}\n1993\t\n1994\t\treturn suffix.String()\n1995\t}\n1996\t\n1997\t// =============================================================================\n1998\t// STREAMLINED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING HELPER\n1999\t// =============================================================================\n2000\t\n2001\t// FormatValidationErrorHelper is a streamlined helper function for formatting validation errors consistently.\n2002\t// This function provides a simple, unified interface for creating validation errors with optional\n2003\t// context, expected/actual values, and custom suggestions.\n2004\t//\n2005\t// This helper is designed to be the primary entry point for validation error formatting,\n2006\t// offering a balance between simplicity and flexibility. It handles the most common validation\n2007\t// error scenarios with a clean, easy-to-use API.\n2008\t//\n2009\t// # Parameters\n2010\t//\n2011\t//   - errorType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n2012\t//     Required. This identifies what kind of validation was performed.\n2013\t//\n2014\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n2015\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2016\t//\n2017\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type)\n2018\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2019\t//\n2020\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization (variadic parameter)\n2021\t//     Use validation helper options like WithContext(), WithSuggestions(), etc.\n2022\t//\n2023\t// # Returns\n2024\t//\n2025\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields that implements\n2026\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or test assertions.\n2027\t//\n2028\t// # Features\n2029\t//\n2030\t//   - **Unified Interface**: Single function for all validation error types\n2031\t//   - **Type Safety**: Works with any value type for expected/actual\n2032\t//   - **Auto-generated Suggestions**: Smart suggestions based on error type and values\n2033\t//   - **Custom Suggestions**: Override with domain-specific suggestions\n2034\t//   - **Flexible Context**: Add contextual information for debugging\n2035\t//   - **Optional Fields**: Only provide what you need for your scenario\n2036\t//\n2037\t// # Common Usage Patterns\n2038\t//\n2039\t// Basic usage with just error type and values:\n2040\t//\n2041\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404)\n2042\t//\n2043\t// With context for debugging:\n2044\t//\n2045\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2046\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2047\t//\n2048\t// With custom suggestions:\n2049\t//\n2050\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"custom_field\", \"required\", \"\",\n2051\t//\t    validate.WithFieldName(\"email\"),\n2052\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Email address is required\", \"Please provide a valid email\"))\n2053\t//\n2054\t// Complete example with all options:\n2055\t//\n2056\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"error_message\",\n2057\t//\t    \"User .* not found\",\n2058\t//\t    \"Internal server error\",\n2059\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"User profile lookup API\"),\n2060\t//\t    validate.WithFieldName(\"error\"),\n2061\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check if user exists\", \"Verify user ID\"))\n2062\t//\n2063\t// # Supported Error Types\n2064\t//\n2065\t// Common error types (auto-recognized for suggestions):\n2066\t//   - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n2067\t//   - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n2068\t//   - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n2069\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n2070\t//   - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n2071\t//   - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n2072\t//   - Custom types: Any string works for domain-specific validation\n2073\t//\n2074\t// # Example Output\n2075\t//\n2076\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2077\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2078\t//\n2079\t//\t// Error() output:\n2080\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n2081\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n2082\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n2083\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n2084\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n2085\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n2086\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n2087\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n2088\t//\n2089\t// # Error Interface Implementation\n2090\t//\n2091\t// The returned ValidationError implements the error interface, so you can:\n2092\t//\n2093\t//\t// Return directly as error\n2094\t//\tif err != nil {\n2095\t//\t    return err\n2096\t//\t}\n2097\t//\n2098\t//\t// Use in logging\n2099\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n2100\t//\n2101\t//\t// Use in test assertions\n2102\t//\tassert.Equal(t, \"expected message\", err.Error())\n2103\t//\n2104\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n2105\t//\n2106\t// If you don't provide custom suggestions, the helper automatically generates them\n2107\t// based on the error type and values:\n2108\t//   - \"status_code\" with 404: Resource and endpoint verification suggestions\n2109\t//   - \"error_message\" with pattern mismatch: Pattern debugging suggestions\n2110\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n2111\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n2112\t//   - Custom types: Generic validation troubleshooting suggestions\n2113\t//\n2114\t// # When to Use This Helper\n2115\t//\n2116\t// Use FormatValidationErrorHelper when you need:\n2117\t//   - A simple, clean API for validation errors\n2118\t//   - Consistent error formatting across your codebase\n2119\t//   - Type-safe error creation with any value types\n2120\t//   - Optional context and suggestions without complexity\n2121\t//   - A unified entry point for all validation error scenarios\n2122\t//\n2123\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control over all fields, use\n2124\t// NewValidationFormatter and the builder pattern instead.\n2125\tfunc FormatValidationErrorHelper(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}, options ...FormatOption) ValidationError {\n2126\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n2127\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n2128\t\t\topt(config)\n2129\t\t}\n2130\t\n2131\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(errorType).\n2132\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n2133\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n2134\t\n2135\t\t// Apply optional configuration\n2136\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n2137\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n2138\t\t}\n2139\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n2140\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n2141\t\t}\n2142\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n2143\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n2144\t\t}\n2145\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n2146\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n2147\t\t}\n2148\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n2149\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n2150\t\t}\n2151\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n2152\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n2153\t\t}\n2154\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n2155\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n2156\t\t}\n2157\t\n2158\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n2159\t}\n2160\t\n2161\t// =============================================================================\n2162\t// VALIDATION ERROR HELPER OPTIONS\n2163\t// operation types, or test scenarios.\n2170\t//\n2171\t// Example usage:\n2172\t//\n2173\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2174\t//\t    validate.WithValidationContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2175\tfunc WithValidationContext(context string) FormatOption {\n2176\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n2177\t\t\tc.Context = {\n2191\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n2192\t\t\t// Note: These would need to be stored separately in FormatConfig\n2193\t\t\t// This is a placeholder for the concept\n2194\t\t}\n2195\t}\n2196\t\n2197\t// =============================================================================\n2198\t// CATEGORY-SPECIFIC FORMATTING FUNCTIONS\n2199\t// =============================================================================\n2200\t\n2201\t// FormatHTTPError creates a formatted error message specifically for HTTP-related errors.\n2202\t// This function applies HTTP-specific formatting with status code details.\n2203\t//\n2204\t// Parameters:\n2205\t//   - errorType: The HTTP error type (e.g., \"status_code\", \"timeout\")\n2206\t//   - [HTTP] [status_code] endpoint: Resource not found (status: 404)\"\n2216\tfunc FormatHTTPError(errorType string, statusCode int, message string, fieldName string) string {\n2217\t\tseverity := GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType)\n2218\t\n2219\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n2220\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatSeverityPrefix(severity))\n2221\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" [HTTP] \")\n2222\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n2223\t\n2224\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", errors.\n2238\t// This function applies validation-specific formatting with field-level focus and severity awareness.\n2239\t//\n2240\t// Parameters:\n2241\t//   - errorType: The validation error type [range] age: Value out of range (expected: 0-120, actual: 150)\"\n2256\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}) string {\n2257\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n2258\t\n2259\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n2260\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatSeverityPrefix(severity))\n2261\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" [Validation] \")\n2262\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n2263\t\n2264\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2265\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n2266\t\t}\n2267\t\n2268\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n2269\t\n2270\t\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\n2271\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n2272\t\t\tif expected != nil \")\n2277\t\t\t}\n2278\t\t\tif actual != nil {\n2279\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", actual))\n2280\t\t\t}\n2281\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n2282\t\t}\n2283\t\n2284\t\treturn builder.String()\n2285\t}\n2286\t\n2287\t// FormatPerformanceError creates a formatted error message specifically for performance errors.\n2288\t// This function applies performance-specific formatting with timing details.\n2289\t//\n2290\t// Parameters:\n2291\t//   - errorType: The performance error \"Request timed out\", \"api_response\")\n2301\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠️ HIGH] [Performance] [timeout] api_response: Request timed out (timeout: 5000ms)\"\n2302\tfunc FormatPerformanceError(errorType strings.Builder\n2306\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatSeverityPrefix(severity))\n2307\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" [Performance] \")\n2308\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \",
